title: Support callback handlers instead of verilated.cpp ifdefs
body:
@gezalore in #3419 suggests:

>being able to register custom handlers for things like vl_fatal and friends by overriding VerilatedContext methods is also something I would favour in contrast to the current ifdefery.

This seems a reasonable idea for v5.  Ones of interest:

#ifdef VL_PRINTF
#ifdef VL_USER_FATAL  ///< Define this to override the vl_fatal function
#ifdef VL_USER_FINISH  ///< Define this to override the vl_finish function
#ifdef VL_USER_STOP  ///< Define this to override the vl_stop function
#ifdef VL_USER_STOP_MAYBE  ///< Define this to override the vl_stop_maybe function
#ifdef VL_USER_WARN  ///< Define this to override the vl_warn function
#ifdef VL_VPRINTF

Rather than callbacks, probably better to call virtual functions in a user provided class that derives from a Verilator-provided base class. (The #ifdefs above predate Verilator even using C++!).  We'd want the functions there to take a VerilatedContext pointer (or have it a member variable?)

I grepped all github projects using Verilator for the above, and 

* VL_PRINTF - many set to just "printf" which was the default anyways, no other settings found
* VL_USER_FATAL - no usages
* VL_USER_FINISH - 70+ overrides, so important
* VL_USER_STOP - opentitan uses
* VL_USER_STOP_MAYBE - no usages
* VL_USER_WARN - no usages
* VL_VPRINTF - no usages

So perhaps we need to make sure to keep VL_USER_FINISH working (unless VL_NO_LEGACY), but we could remove the rest and throw errors on if used.
